strengths: Demonstrates responsive design considerations, reflecting effective ATL application
Exhibits strong communication skills through iterative client feedback and design adjustments
Provides a detailed account of the research process, citing multiple sources and reflections
Includes prototype screenshots that illustrate an iterative development process
weaknesses: Does not document how the plan was adjusted when tasks slipped, limiting evidence of organizational skill
Fails to explicitly link research insights to specific design or functional decisions
Includes redundant planning tables instead of streamlined updates highlighting changes
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student effectively applied ATL skills in research, communication, and design, supported by concrete examples and artifacts. Strengthening this section will require documenting adaptations when timelines shifted, directly tying research findings to design choices, and streamlining documentation to showcase organizational practices rather than repeating tables.

strengths: Justifies focus on homepage, content, design, and functionality with user-experience research
Clearly connects the learning goal to personal interest and client context
Includes a testing phase to validate functionality
Provides a detailed, logically sequenced timeline with meetings and milestones
weaknesses: Fails to specify the technology stack and frameworks for the product
Uses vague success metrics (e.g., âasking peopleâ) instead of defined instruments
Does not break down each criterion into measurable sub-criteria or targets
Lacks milestone markers to track progress and adjust plans
Success criteria lack specific, measurable details
Mobile-responsive requirements omit defined breakpoints or performance benchmarks
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning shows strong grounding in user-experience research and a meaningful link between the studentâs interests and the client context, supported by a clear timeline and a testing phase. To move forward, the plan needs more technical specificity and objective measures: defining technologies, sub-criteria, milestones, and performance targets will make goals measurable and ensure progress can be tracked effectively.

strengths: Offers a candid reflection on time-management challenges, showing genuine self-evaluation
weaknesses: Evaluation table restates criteria without evidential support such as user feedback or analytics
Legend colors are described but not visually applied or linked to commentary
Concludes product success without supporting evidence like user quotes or survey results
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflection demonstrates honest insight into personal learning challenges, particularly in time management. However, the evaluation of the product against success criteria needs concrete evidenceâactual user data, aligned visual indicators, and specific feedbackâto substantiate claims and provide a robust assessment of impact on learning and product quality.

annotations.comment: Specify the technology stack and frameworks you will use (e.g., HTML, CSS, hosting platform) to make your product plan more concrete and feasible.
Your justification for focusing on homepage, content, design, and functionality is well grounded in user-experience research, showing thoughtful planning.
Demonstrating responsive design considerations shows effective ATL application in addressing diverse user contexts.
Your communication ATL skill is evident: you iteratively gathered client feedback and adjusted design details based on specific comments.
Reflect on how you adjusted your plan when tasks slippedâdocumenting plan adaptations strengthens your organization skill evidence.
You provide a detailed account of your research process, citing multiple sources and reflection on learningâstrong evidence of ATL skill application.
You clearly connect your learning goal to personal interest and client context, demonstrating strong motivation and relevance for the project.
âAsking peopleâ is vague for measuring success. Define specific metrics or feedback instruments (e.g., survey questions, usability tests) for clear evaluation.
Break down each product criterion into sub-criteria and measurable targets to clarify expectations and streamline evaluation.
Including a testing phase for functionality demonstrates foresight and enhances the feasibility of your planning.
Your timeline is detailed and sequenced logically, with clear meetings and milestones that support steady progress.
Consider adding milestone markers in your planning section so you can track progress at key points and adjust as needed.
You outline the website success criteria well; next, specify measurable details for each to ensure they can be objectively assessed.
Your mobile-responsive criterion lacks breakpoints or performance targets. Define specific device widths or usability benchmarks for clarity.
Your research insights are rich; link each insight to specific design or functional decisions to show direct impact on your product.
Including screenshots of your prototype supports an iterative development process and illustrates how research informed design.
Avoid repeating the same planning table; streamline documentation to highlight updates and changes as part of organizational practice.
Your candid reflection on time-management challenges shows genuine insight into your learning process and self-evaluation.
The evaluation table restates criteria but lacks evidence. Include actual user feedback or analytics data to substantiate each assessment.
The legend colors are noted without application. Ensure visual indicators in your evaluation align with your commentary for clarity.
You conclude that the product is successful but provide no supporting evidence. Add user quotes or survey results to validate this judgment.
